title: Syrian Herbal Flatbread
description: A delicate and indulgent Syrian flatbread flavored with a variety of herbs and spices, perfect for a quick and easy lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: instant yeast may also be used

ingredients.name: sumac
ingredients.notes: optional, but recommended for a traditional Syrian flavor

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and black pepper.
Add the warm water and yeast to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let the dough sit in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) with a baking stone or a cast-iron skillet inside.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on how large you want your flatbreads to be.
Roll out each piece of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Transfer the dough to the preheated baking stone or skillet and bake for 2-3 minutes, or until the dough is lightly browned and puffed.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and brush them with olive oil.
Sprinkle the parsley, thyme, and sumac over the flatbreads, then top with sesame seeds if using.
Return the flatbreads to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the herbs are fragrant and the flatbreads are golden brown.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
